# A single file mixing one unframed route and one htmx(...)-framed route (no
# flag). Behavior proves the split: the framed route renders with a type that
# carries .HXRedirect, the unframed route renders normally. muxt check passing
# proves both routes' template-data types exist and typecheck.

muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /plain Plain(ctx)" -}}<p>{{- .Result -}}</p>{{- end -}}
{{- define "GET /framed htmx(Framed(ctx))" -}}{{- .HXRedirect "/done" -}}<p>{{- .Result -}}</p>{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.24
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

func (Server) Plain(ctx context.Context) string  { return "plain" }
func (Server) Framed(ctx context.Context) string { return "framed" }
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestPlainRouteRendersNormally(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/plain", nil))
	if got := rec.Body.String(); !strings.Contains(got, "plain") {
		t.Fatalf("/plain body = %q, want it to contain %q", got, "plain")
	}
	if got := rec.Header().Get("HX-Redirect"); got != "" {
		t.Fatalf("/plain set HX-Redirect = %q, want none", got)
	}
}

func TestFramedRouteUsesHTMXHelpers(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/framed", nil))
	if got := rec.Header().Get("HX-Redirect"); got != "/done" {
		t.Fatalf("/framed HX-Redirect = %q, want /done", got)
	}
	if got := rec.Body.String(); !strings.Contains(got, "framed") {
		t.Fatalf("/framed body = %q, want it to contain %q", got, "framed")
	}
}
